mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 8.5 percent within two months signals a labor market recovery that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous housing starts exceeding 600 thousand units and a decline in residential mortgage debt indicate a deleveraging housing sector that reallocates capital toward consumer goods and equities, lifting forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over the 60-day horizon in more than half of historical episodes, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate profit margins, which directly supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 2.0% annualized for two consecutive months, indicating that supply expansion is being offset by demand destruction.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5 percent annualized triggers a recessionary risk premium that compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or if the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a consumer price index rise above 3.0 percent annualized signals an overheating economy that forces aggressive monetary tightening, ultimately depressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both series breach thresholds simultaneously, or the joint predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in residential mortgage debt outstanding reflects expanding household credit availability and wealth effects that directly support consumer spending and equity demand, generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when RSAFS grows at an annualized rate above 4.0%, indicating a decoupling of credit growth from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the trailing twelve-month CPI inflation rate, real policy rates turn positive and tighten financial conditions, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional information coefficient remains below 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.
